mission.title: Install and Configure ClickHouse
mission.description: ClickHouse is a real-time analytics database management system designed for generating analytical data reports. It is ideal for applications requiring high performance and scalability in data processing. Key trade-offs include the need for careful schema design and potential complexity in query optimization.

steps.title: Add ClickHouse Helm Repository
steps.description:
This step adds the ClickHouse Helm repository to your local Helm configuration, allowing you to install ClickHouse using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add clickhouse https://clickhouse.github.io/clickhouse-operator

```

> This is the official Helm chart repository for ClickHouse.

steps.title: Install ClickHouse Operator
steps.description:
This step installs the ClickHouse operator, which manages ClickHouse clusters on Kubernetes.

```bash

helm install clickhouse-operator clickhouse/clickhouse-operator --namespace clickhouse --create-namespace --version 0.24.0

```

> Ensure you are using the correct version of the ClickHouse operator.

steps.title: Create ClickHouse Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a ClickHouse cluster using a custom resource definition (CRD).

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: clickhouse.altinity.com/v1
kind: ClickHouseCluster
metadata:
  name: example-cluster
  namespace: clickhouse
spec:
  layout: replicated
  templates:
    podTemplate:
      spec:
        containers:
          - name: clickhouse
            image: clickhouse/clickhouse-server:latest
  shards:
    - name: shard1
      replicas:
        - name: replica1
EOF

```

> This YAML creates a simple replicated ClickHouse cluster.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: clickhouse.altinity.com/v1
kind: ClickHouseCluster
metadata:
  name: example-cluster
  namespace: clickhouse
spec:
  layout: replicated
  templates:
    podTemplate:
      spec:
        containers:
          - name: clickhouse
            image: clickhouse/clickhouse-server:latest
  shards:
    - name: shard1
      replicas:
        - name: replica1
EOF

steps.title: Verify ClickHouse Cluster
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the ClickHouse cluster to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get clickhousecluster -n clickhouse

```

> You should see the status of your ClickHouse cluster.

uninstall.title: Uninstall ClickHouse Operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the ClickHouse operator and all associated resources. Be aware that this will delete the ClickHouse cluster and all data within it.

```bash

helm uninstall clickhouse-operator --namespace clickhouse

kubectl delete namespace clickhouse

```

> This command will remove the operator and its resources.
> This command will delete the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade ClickHouse Operator
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your data. This step will upgrade the ClickHouse operator to a newer version.

```bash

kubectl drain <node-name> --ignore-daemonsets

helm upgrade clickhouse-operator clickhouse/clickhouse-operator --namespace clickhouse --version 0.25.0

kubectl uncordon <node-name>

kubectl get clickhousecluster -n clickhouse

```

> Drain the node to safely upgrade.
> Upgrade to the specified version.
> Uncordon the node after the upgrade.
> Verify the cluster status after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create ClickHouse cluster'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur due to insufficient permissions or misconfigured CRD.

**Fix:**
Check the RBAC permissions and ensure the CRD is correctly defined.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Pod is in CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This usually indicates an issue with the ClickHouse configuration or resource limits.

**Fix:**
Inspect the pod logs using 'kubectl logs <pod-name>' and adjust the configuration or resource requests.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Connection refused'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can happen if the ClickHouse server is not running or the service is misconfigured.

**Fix:**
Verify the status of the ClickHouse pods and services with 'kubectl get pods -n clickhouse' and 'kubectl get svc -n clickhouse'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Kubernetes cluster does not have enough resources to schedule the ClickHouse pods.

**Fix:**
Increase the resources available in your Kubernetes cluster or adjust the resource requests in your ClickHouse configuration.

versionNotes.changes: Introduced support for new data types and improved query performance.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated the old replication method in favor of a new, more efficient one.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v25.x, ensure to migrate your data to the new replication method.

resolution.summary: You should see the ClickHouse cluster listed with 'kubectl get clickhousecluster -n clickhouse' showing the status as 'Running'.

EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with sufficient resources.
